entries.title: Exposed backups and source files
entries.body:
Developers and servers spawn side copies of files: editor swaps (vim `.swp`), saves (`~`, `.orig`), manual backups before an edit (`config.php.bak`), release archives (`backup.zip`, `site.tar.gz`), DB dumps (`db.sql`). If such a file sits in the webroot and the server serves it as TEXT (instead of executing it), the source code leaks together with its secrets.

Impact: DB creds and API keys from configs, app logic and hidden endpoints, a chain to RCE or DB access. Often an easy high-severity finding.

Two separate surfaces: (1) a backup of a KNOWN file (`/config.php` -> `/config.php.bak`), (2) an archive/dump of the whole project in the root (`/backup.zip`, `/www.tar.gz`, `/dump.sql`). Related: exposed `.git`/`.svn` (see Insecure Source Code Management) and `.env` (see API Key Leaks).

entries.title: Backup copy suffixes
entries.body: swp swo bak old orig save tmp dist backup zip tar gz sql

rows: vim swap files for an open file (often `.index.php.swp`)
versions left during a manual update
shipped config template (often with default creds)
file 'turned off' by renaming, so it stopped executing

entries.title: Common names and paths
entries.body:
Base = current file + suffix:
  /config.php -> /config.php.bak, /config.php~, /config.php.old, /config.bak
  /index.php  -> /index.php.swp, /.index.php.swp

CMS configs (candidates for a backup suffix):
  WordPress: /wp-config.php
  Drupal:    /sites/default/settings.php
  Joomla:    /configuration.php
  Magento:   /app/etc/env.php
  Laravel:   /.env
  generic:   /config.php /database.php /db.php /conn.php /settings.php

Whole-project archive/dump in the root (by domain name and common words):
  /backup.zip /backup.tar.gz /www.zip /site.tar.gz /web.zip
  /example.com.zip /example.zip /public_html.tar.gz
  /db.sql /dump.sql /database.sql /backup.sql

Ready wordlists: SecLists `Discovery/Web-Content/` (`backup-files.txt`, `BackupFiles.fuzz.txt`, `coldfusion`), toolkit `juicy_files.txt` / `backup_files_only.txt`.

entries.title: Fuzzing backup files (ffuf / feroxbuster)
entries.body:
# 1) Clusterbomb: known files x suffixes (two fuzz points)
ffuf -w files.txt:FILE -w exts.txt:EXT -u 'http://target.htb/FILEEXT' -mc 200 -ac
#   files.txt: config.php\nindex.php\ndatabase.php …
#   exts.txt:  .bak\n.old\n.swp\n~\n.save\n.zip …

# 2) Archive/dump of the whole project in the root
ffuf -w archive-names.txt -u 'http://target.htb/FUZZ' -e .zip,.tar.gz,.sql,.bak -mc 200 -ac

# 3) feroxbuster with auto-appended extensions
feroxbuster -u http://target.htb -x bak,old,swp,zip,sql,tar.gz -w common.txt

# 4) point check on a found path
curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code} %{size_download}\n' http://target.htb/config.php.bak

# -ac (auto-calibrate) filters 'soft 200s' (custom 404s). For .swp:
#   vim -r .config.php.swp   # recover the original from the swap

entries.title: Tell source disclosure from a normal page
entries.body:
The goal is not '200 OK', it is 'the server returned SOURCE instead of executing it'. Signs:
• `Content-Type: text/plain` (or `application/octet-stream`) on something normally served as html.
• Code visible in the body: `<?php`, `<?=`, `$_GET[`, `$_POST[`, `define('DB_`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `'password' =>`, DB connection strings.
• Response size clearly differs from the normal page of the same name.

For an archive/dump: `Content-Type: application/zip` / `application/sql`, magic bytes at the start (`PK` for zip, `SQLite format` for sqlite). Download and grep for `password`, `secret`, `api_key`, `BEGIN PRIVATE KEY`.

entries.title: Wayback and nuclei for backups
entries.body:
# Historical backups from the web archive (see Commands -> Recon -> Wayback CDX)
curl -s 'https://web.archive.org/cdx/search/cdx?url=*.example.com/*&collapse=urlkey&output=text&fl=original&filter=original:.*\.(zip|tar\.gz|sql|bak|old|backup|swp|env)$' | sort -u

# nuclei: ready templates for backup files and dumps
nuclei -u http://target.htb -tags backup,exposure -severity medium,high

# gobuster pattern-mode: substitute the domain into archive name templates
gobuster dir -u http://target.htb -w common.txt -x zip,tar.gz,sql,bak

# Note: downloading someone's full DB dump out of scope is risky -
# confirm minimally (header/first bytes), clear a full pull against program rules.

entries.title: Prevention
entries.body:
• Do not keep backups and dumps in the webroot; back up outside DocumentRoot.
• Deny serving service extensions at the server level (nginx/apache `location ~* \.(bak|old|swp|save|orig|sql|zip|tar\.gz)$ { deny all; }`).
• Clean editor swap files on prod (`.*.swp`); do not edit files directly on production.
• Do not ship `.env`/`config.*.dist` with real secrets into a public folder.
• Monitor 200 responses on typical backup paths (a rare but precise leak signal).
